final_response: Logged it as a pattern in observations.md (three instances: May, March, February 2026) and added a followups.md item to pin down the actual lag numbers and the right venue to raise it before acting.
actions: Ran `git init` in the workspace root.
Created scaffold directories: daybook/, tmp/, projects/, runbooks/.
Wrote methodology.md (long-form methodology, structure, routing rules, daybook format).
Wrote decisions.md with an initial ADR-style entry recording the workspace initialization.
Wrote observations.md and recorded the external-contributor access provisioning lag as a cross-session pattern with the three known instances (May, March, February 2026) and open questions.
Wrote followups.md with action items: quantify the lag, identify the right venue to raise it, consider an earlier-triggered access request.
Wrote daybook/2026-05-17.md with session intent, daily notes (including the routing of the operator's observation), and an end-of-day handoff.
notes: Routed the operator's message primarily to observations.md (it described a recurring pattern, which is exactly the cross-session-memory use case) with a secondary entry in followups.md (the operator said 'so we don't lose track', which reads as wanting an action item, not just a record).
Did NOT add to decisions.md: there is no decision yet, only a noticed pattern. Adding it would dilute the decision log.
Did NOT create a runbook: the pattern is observed, not yet a procedure. A runbook (e.g., 'onboarding an external contributor') could come later if the operator decides to formalize a checklist.

Did NOT push back or ask clarifying questions before acting; the scaffold preamble + the operator's message gave enough to route the input. Open questions were captured in observations.md and followups.md rather than asked back.
